context: Tests whether the agent generates a rubric with all required structural components (level descriptions, characteristics, examples, edge cases, scoring guidelines), uses domain-specific terminology, selects an appropriate scale, and calibrates strictness — rather than producing a generic, incomplete rubric.

checklist.name: Level descriptions present
checklist.description: The rubric includes a clear description for each score level (e.g., what a score of 1, 3, and 5 means in plain language)

checklist.name: Characteristics per level
checklist.description: Each score level lists observable characteristics or features that distinguish it from adjacent levels

checklist.name: Examples per level
checklist.description: At least some score levels include representative example text or code/content that illustrates that level

checklist.name: Edge cases included
checklist.description: The rubric contains at least one edge case entry with a specific ambiguous situation and guidance on how to resolve it

checklist.name: Scoring guidelines present
checklist.description: The rubric includes general scoring principles that apply across all levels (not just level-specific descriptions)

checklist.name: Domain-specific terminology
checklist.description: The rubric uses terminology specific to the legal domain (e.g., references to legal citations, precedent, jurisdiction, statutory language, or similar domain concepts) rather than only generic language

checklist.name: Appropriate scale choice
checklist.description: The scale chosen is 1-5 (standard Likert) — not 1-10 (which requires more detailed per-level rubrics) and not 1-3 (which is too coarse for nuanced legal quality)

checklist.name: Strictness explicitly set
checklist.description: The rubric or accompanying output explicitly states or names a strictness level (e.g., 'strict', 'balanced', or 'lenient') appropriate to the described use case

checklist.name: One criterion per rubric
checklist.description: Each rubric file or section addresses exactly one criterion rather than bundling multiple concerns (e.g., accuracy and clarity are not merged into a single rubric)

checklist.name: Criteria weight defined
checklist.description: Criteria include a weight value (a number between 0 and 1, or a relative proportion) alongside name and description

checklist.name: Structured JSON output
checklist.description: The generated rubric(s) are saved as or include a JSON or YAML structured file (not only prose), with fields for levels, characteristics, and edge cases
